DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL KEMPEL'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET THREE; REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS CCP 2030, 2031. MOTION GRANTED IN PART; DENIED IN PART. AS TO RFPD NO. 58, DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE ANY REPORTS THAT IT RAN (OR CAN RUN) USING SOFTWARE THAT REFLECTS THE WEBSITES THAT WERE VISITED BY DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES. DEFENDANT IS NOT REQUIRED TO CHECK THE INDIVIDUAL COMPUTERS OF EACH EMPLOYEE. TRULY PRIVATE AND CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION CAN BE REDACTED BUT MUST BE LOGGED. IF THERE IS A DISPUTE REGARDING THE REDACTIONS, THE REPORTS SHALL BE SUBMITTED TO THE COURT FOR AN IN-CAMERA REVIEW. THE MOTION AS TO RFPD NOS. 60 AND 61 IS DENIED AS THE COURT FINDS THAT THEY ARE OVERBROAD AND THE INFORMATION CAN BE OBTAINED IN LESS BURDENSOME MEANS SUCH AS DEPOSITIONS OR SPECIFIC INTERROGATORIES. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A DATE CERTAIN FOR DEFENDANT TO COMPLETE ITS PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS BY AUGUST 12, 2019. THE REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR APR-6-2020 IS CONTINUED TO MASTER CALENDAR JURY ON AUG-03-2020 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. JEANNETTE VACCARO, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF, SUMY KIM, ESQ. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three; Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs Ccp 2030, 2031
Off Calendar Jul-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MICHAEL KEMPEL's Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three; Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs Ccp 2030, 2031.

Motion granted in part; denied in part. As to RFPD No. 58, Defendant shall produce any reports that it ran (or can run) using software that reflects the websites that were visited by Defendant's employees. Defendant is not required to check the individual computers of each employee. Truly private and confidential information can be redacted but must be logged. If there is a dispute regarding the redactions, the reports shall be submitted to the Court for an in-camera review. The motion as to RFPD Nos. 60 and 61 is denied as the Court finds that they are overbroad and the information can be obtained in less burdensome means such as depositions or specific interrogatories. Plaintiff's request for a date certain for Defendant to complete its production of documents is granted. Defendant shall produce all responsive documents by August 12, 2019. The requests for sanctions are denied.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
